title: Crystalline Peruvian Soybean Casserole
description: A resinous, flaky casserole that blends Peruvian flavors with soybeans, creating a nostalgic and flexitarian-friendly dish.
instructions: Rinse soybeans and soak them in water overnight or for at least 8 hours.
Drain soaked beans and place them in a pot with 4 cups water; bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until tender, about 45 minutes.
While beans cook, heat olive oil in a wok over medium heat and sauté onion, garlic, and red bell pepper until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add cooked beans, coconut milk, soy sauce, cumin, and smoked paprika to the wok; stir to combine.
Season mixture with salt and pepper and cook for another 10 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Preheat oven to 375°F and line a baking dish with parchment paper.
Transfer bean mixture to the baking dish, smooth the top, and sprinkle panko breadcrumbs evenly.
Bake for 20 minutes until breadcrumbs are golden and the casserole is bubbling.
Remove from oven, let rest 5 minutes, then garnish with chopped cilantro before serving.
